proposed_base_material_variables.nonmetallic_inclusion_profile: Specific quantification and characterization of nonmetallic inclusions, such as bulk oxygen parts-per-million, sulfides, and oxides, which act as preferential initiation sites for localized corrosion.

proposed_conditioned_material_variables.specimen_dimensions: The explicit physical dimensions and geometric shape of the test coupon (e.g., length, width, thickness) extracted for the experiment prior to mounting or masking.

proposed_experiment_variables.applied_potential: The constant electrical potential explicitly applied and maintained on the working electrode during potentiostatic tests, such as when determining critical pitting or crevice temperatures.
proposed_experiment_variables.critical_pitting_temperature: The lowest explicitly measured temperature at which stable localized pitting corrosion initiates on the material surface under a constant applied potential.
proposed_experiment_variables.critical_crevice_temperature: The lowest explicitly measured temperature at which stable crevice corrosion initiates under a constant applied potential when an artificial crevice is present.
proposed_experiment_variables.temperature_scan_protocol: The defined continuous heating rate or stepwise temperature increment and dwell time (e.g., 2 degrees Celsius every 15 minutes) applied to the electrolyte during critical pitting/crevice temperature testing.
proposed_experiment_variables.crevice_prevention_technique: The specialized apparatus or procedure utilized to suppress unwanted crevice corrosion at the electrode-mounting interface, such as an inert gas (argon) bubble or flushed-port design.
proposed_experiment_variables.crevice_former_details: The material, geometric configuration, and application method (e.g., clamped metal-to-metal coupon) of the artificial crevice creator used specifically for critical crevice temperature testing.
